text: In this letter, we propose a practical non-linear energy harvesting model and design an algorithm for resource allocation in SWIPT systems. The optimization is non-convex due to non-linear power conversion at energy harvesting receivers and the constraints on the SIRs at the information receivers. In this letter, we formulate the optimization problem of maximizing the sum of power harvested at EH receivers, given that all the SIRs at the information receivers are constrained above certain thresholds. We rewrite the sum-of-ratios non-convex objective function into an equivalent subtractive form and use the resultant function to obtain an efficient iterative algorithm for resource allocation. An optimal rank constrained semidefinite program (SDP) is solved at each iteration of the algorithm by applying the SDP relaxation. The simulations validate that significant gains can be achieved by designing the resource allocation based on the proposed non-linear energy harvesting model rather than the linear model.
